programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden is preoccupied with the conjoined twins and Jayne arranges for their transfer to a hospital in London. Daisha discovers her pregnancy is beyond the legal limit for abortions and starts to consider ways to get rid of her unborn child - much to Mark's horror. After spending time with a young patient and the arrival of her fiance's sister, Faye rethinks her engagement to Joseph and their childless future

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack and Tony put their differences aside as they say their goodbyes to Eddie, while Lara and Luke are forced to acknowledge they may have been wrong about one another. Simon's bad mood makes him less than sympathetic toward staff and patients alike, and matters come to a head when Colette breaks the news to him that her child will have learning difficulties

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey's partying catches up with him when he wakes up in a strange bed and ends up being grilled at Mill Street station on rape charges. Will takes a blood sample from an unconscious driver who has knocked a courier off his bike, and takes advice from Matt about whether it is admissible evidence in court. When the sample goes missing, he jumps to conclusions about his love-rival's determination to make life difficult for him

programme.desc: It's the longest night of David's life when he takes Bianca on a driving lesson. And Cindy goes looking for a diversion after a night from hell with Ian

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel makes some last minute preparations for the final while Debbie gets some unwanted attention at work. Cindy is in a state of shock

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Napier is intrigued by an ex-colonial couple recently returned from India who settle in Tannochbrae and refuse to have contact with their mysterious daughter. Starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew tries to convince his friend Ronnie Neale that he has a serious illness, an unenviable task at the best of times and one complicated by the prospect of losing Dawn to her estranged husband Keith. Meanwhile, confusion abounds as Sam and Joanna are called out separately to the same patient. Starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Sarah Parish

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Angus Grier's moment of triumph proves short-lived as James stumbles on his guilty secret, while Tristan faces up to telling Siegfried about his exam results, and thinks a lifestyle change might help get his brother back on side

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester thinks William has been unfaithful when he comes home from work reeking of perfume. He offers her a perfectly innocent explanation, but she continues to fear the worst. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana practise their rather inadequate midwifery skills by helping Sarah deliver her baby. Graham Crowden, Stephanie Cole and Lucy Aston star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle enlists Rene to set up a mobile radio station to broadcast propaganda, the parts of which will be dropped by plane. Unfortunately, they fall down one of the chateau's chimneys. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard finds himself with some explaining to do after Pearl discovers an incriminating long blonde hair on his person, so he turns to his friends for help. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part one of two. The green-fingered amateur sleuths are drawn into another mystery when a barrister employing them to spruce up his garden discovers his cousin has been drowned, only for the man to be spotted alive and well a few days later. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems somebody has taken the island's Day of the Dead celebrations a little too literally when a man receives a voicemail from his wife saying she is being attacked. When she turns up dead, suspicion falls upon her friends and acquaintances at the nearby yacht club - her husband Finn, club secretary Charlotte and chairman Hugh. However, Jack's curiosity is piqued by a yellow butterfly found on the victim's clothing and he calls upon Commissioner Selwyn's knowledge of such matters. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring John Sessions and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Danny and Fiona investigate a safe house they suspect is being used by North African terrorists. However, while planting listening devices their cover is blown and they are captured, leaving colleagues at MI5 with a difficult dilemma. The terrorists are demanding nothing less than the complete withdrawal of British forces from Iraq, or they will kill the agents. Adam's concern for his wife clouds his judgement and he agrees to negotiate, not realising he is walking into a trap

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey's partying catches up with him when he wakes up in a strange bed and ends up being grilled at Mill Street station on rape charges. Will takes a blood sample from an unconscious driver who has knocked a courier off his bike, and takes advice from Matt about whether it is admissible evidence in court. When the sample goes missing, he jumps to conclusions about his love-rival's determination to make life difficult for him

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: Confederate belle Emma is overwhelmed by her friend Tom's mental state. Dr Foster wrestles with his marriage and career as Mary tries to improve her patients' lives with Samuel's help. Aurelia submits to Silas's advances in exchange for his help

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William and Hester devise an escape plan when Emma's overbearing in-laws invite themselves over to dinner and to stay the weekend. programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's complaints of rising damp fall on deaf ears when Diana is notified her high-yield, high-risk securities are in receivership. Harvey makes a suggestion that he hopes will address both issues. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Resistance leader Michelle blackmails Von Strohm and Gruber for 10 million francs, after revealing she has a photograph of the pair holding the Fallen Madonna, while Herr Flick gives Helga a truth drug to discover the location of the painting

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A solicitor's letter addressed to Compo causes a stir in the town - but terrifies his son Tom. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Tom Owen

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A newlywed, recently arrived in England from New Zealand, sees disturbing visions of a strangled corpse in the hallway of her dream home. Joan Hickson stars as the amateur detective determined to get to the bottom of the mystery

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: As the Gibraltar locals reel from the murder of shipping agent Gordon Fletcher, Brian and Gerry - last seen being carried off in a shipping container - find themselves stranded on the side of a Spanish mountain, and now face the problem of how to get back to the rest of the team. As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. Conclusion of a two-part story, with regulars Amanda Redman, Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson joined by Vincent Regan and George Irving

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Danny's colleagues gather for his funeral, but the service is interrupted by news of a bomb blast in central London. The team races against time to prevent a second catastrophe, and comes under the scrutiny of a National Security co-ordinator with a grudge against Harry

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Honey is horrified to learn Matt has taken over all the wedding arrangements and warns Emma not to rush blindly into marriage. programme.desc: Dr Foster confronts his family's divided loyalties when his mother and wounded Confederate brother arrive. Alice is shocked to find her fiance, Tom, deeply changed by war. Mary Elizabeth Winstead, Josh Radnor and AnnaSophia Robb star

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

Here, his much-needed Highland walking holiday ends in the former, but it still turns out to be pretty hectic. David Rintoul stars

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: The sickly folk of Cardale are hit by yet another mystery illness, which sweeps through the village spreading terror in its wake _ and poor old Andrew, whose only desire is to get away from it all, finds himself drawn into deadly danger. Gary Mavers stars in the medical drama

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James performs an unfamiliar operation on a pedigree bull and helps his partners treat Mrs Bond's cats. Yorkshire veterinary drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester joins a sponsored cycle race to Brighton in aid of the RSPCA, but risks finishing up in worse shape than most of the animals she is trying to help. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: After moving into a double room, Tom and Diana bicker about whose furniture they should use. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: It's Rene's birthday and surprises include a car from Edith, a wink from Hitler and a bomb from the Resistance. The celebration concludes with a bang

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry's promotion at the building society gives him a headache when he has to visit a particularly violent family - and he naively asks Clegg and Truly for advice. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Mike Grady

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Customs seize a massive heroin shipment and Kavanagh agrees to prosecute the importer, a self-made man with all the trappings of success, including a yacht and a villa in Portugal. Unfortunately, he also has a staff of hired heavies who make the mistake of threatening Lizzie in a bid to get him off the hook - thus setting the scene for a bitterly personal courtroom wrangle. John Thaw and Lisa Harrow star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 16-year-old case of an infamous getaway driver convicted of murdering his wife when new evidence suggests it could have been a miscarriage of justice. But Gerry, who was on the original investigation, refuses to believe the prisoner is anything but guilty. Meanwhile, Brian is in a reflective mood, convinced his days at Ucos are numbered after his actions against former police commander Embleton. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star as the veteran coppers, with Amanda Redman as their boss

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zafar and Adam race against the clock to uncover the latest bomb, while the power struggle between Juliet and Harry intensifies. As a new ultimatum is issued and Tash mysteriously disappears, the team is forced to deal with the possibility of a mole within the organisation. Guest starring Martine McCutcheon and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates the disappearance of a prostitute whose evidence helped put away rapist Martin Delaney. Zain finally persuades Kristen Shaw to name her supplier and puts himself in great danger to arrange a meeting with the mysterious Jose Alvarez. Phil apologises to Sam for missing their date and agrees to take things slowly, but Stuart makes it clear he's not going to be pushed out without a fight

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: During a Union ball, the Green girls and Frank initiate a plan to help Tom escape. Dr Foster guides Samuel through an operation

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star

programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Angela Rippon reveal how banks are leaving some victims of fraud high and dry, despite new protections having been introduced

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell hear from the new owners of a house in Erdington, Birmingham, and six holiday homes in an old vicarage in Llanfynydd, Carmarthenshire

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special edition of the programme exploring what it takes to police Britain's roads, including footage of a Metropolitan Police operation to tackle moped-enabled crime

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An Aberdeen crew on nightshift searches for a woman at risk of hypothermia, while the air ambulance transfers a patient from his home on Lewis to Glasgow for hospital treatment

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster, Herefordshire, where experts Tim Weeks and Kate Bliss help two teams search for antiques that they hope will make them a profit at auction

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The return of Basil and Robert Thomas - the sons of a local mine owner - causes Prem and his wife Kamini to face the prospect of becoming outcasts once again, while others welcome the duo with open arms. Drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's knack for gardening as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo also look at the country's passion for growing vegetables at home, a skill that has inspired some amazing dishes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a property in Oxfordshire on a budget of £900,000, and finds out about charcoal-making the environmentally friendly way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and David and Harry Rich transform a garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex, into the perfect place for John and David to host their wedding reception

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of wine, and a retro tin toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda worries that Mick is being distant so goes to find Tina, who reassures her that his behaviour has nothing to do with her. Ben shares his concerns with Callum about Phil seeing Raymond, and what will happen if Denise finds out - but unknown to them, Lexi overhears their conversation. Gray encourages Whitney to tell the truth in her final statements, while Jean and Kat are shocked by a photo of Ruby and Martin on social media, and try to keep the news from Stacey

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they can't resist getting involved with Thomas's tale as well

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Birmingham, recalling the colourful characters he met while working the door at Digbeth's hottest night spots. He then introduces three guests, with Bella Hull making her TV debut with some excellent time saving tips, Texas-born Kemah Bob discussing the differences between Americans and Brits, and Chris Washington filling the audience in on lockdown fitness pursuits

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets a man whose search for a Hollywood smile has left him thousands of pounds out of pocket, and vulnerable people paying for warranties that are worthless

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a listed cottage in the Merseyside village of Port Sunlight, Lucy Alexander explores a Derbyshire hotel, and Martin Roberts looks round a Stoke-on-Trent terrace

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Special episode looking at knife crime in the UK, including a dedicated police task force in Essex and a groundbreaking scheme in the West Midlands

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Ambulance crew Steve and John assess a patient in Glasgow who has suffered a suspected stroke, but she is reluctant to go to hospital. Colin and Dan deal with possible sepsis

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles talks about Toby jugs with a young collector

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: As the Thomas brothers begin trying to convince the villagers to back their plan for a new town, Aled and Daf decide they need to do something big to stop Basil's development plan. Prem tells Owen that he cannot work in the mine anymore because of his health, and Megan calls at the surgery with some unexpected news

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore food-related customs associated with wedding ceremonies. The duo help prepare a Scottish breakfast fit for a bride and groom, and highlight traditions behind the star of the nuptial feast - the cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a family home in the Shropshire countryside. While in the area, Jules visits Acton Scott estate, home to an authentic Victorian working farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create an exotic garden inspired by tropical rainforests for a family in Ruddington, Nottingham

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze racehorse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda helps a guilty Gray by picking up the kids from school and cleaning up his home - only to get on his bad side by touching Chantelle's favourite mug. In the meantime, Whitney's QC is taken ill at court, meaning there is only one other person who can step up for the final statements. Lola questions Ben about Phil's relationship to Raymond - but when he tells her to stay out of it, she feels torn, and later decides to tell Denise. Stacey finds herself in an embarrassing situation

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The High Court enforcement officers face an angry car dealer in Peterborough, while Rob and Gerald force a curry restaurant in Manchester to close early. Andy plans to remove two boats from Falmouth Harbour to cover an unpaid debt, and the sheriffs visit a pub on the Isle of Wight to retrieve money on behalf of a former employee. Narrated by David Reed

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The neighbours all confront a truth in their life. David investigates Kelly's past, unsure of what he's hoping to find. Can he assuage his doubts, or will he always fear that she wasn't the woman he thought she was? An argument with Liam propels Hannah toward Andy, but their unexpected moment of intimacy is interrupted. Belle's ex-husband Neil walks back into her life and tests her loyalty to Maya, on the night when Maya needs her most. Gail finally tells Henry her true feelings about their relationship in an explosive confrontation, which dissects their marriage from its very beginnings

programme.title: Being British East Asian: Sex, Beauty & Bodies
programme.desc: Elaine Chong lifts the lid on how many British East Asians feel being caught between cultures of West and East. In this first episode, she looks at love and sex

programme.title: Being British East Asian: Sex, Beauty & Bodies
programme.desc: Elaine Chong considers beauty and plastic surgery

programme.title: Being British East Asian: Sex, Beauty & Bodies
programme.desc: Elaine Chong explores weight and body image

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness each buy a sporty insurance write-off for £6,000 and take part in three challenges at the programme's test track before they're ordered to make their cars road legal and report to Alexandra Palace, where the producers have constructed a monstrous Wall of Death. The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Thirty minutes into the night shift, police request urgent assistance at the scene of an incident - a man has called to say his mum has been stabbed. On arrival paramedics find she is in cardiac arrest, and she is declared the sickest patient in London by Ambulance Control. Across town, Josh and Vinny are dealing with more practical issues. They've been dispatched to their first patient of the night - a woman who's collapsed in a casino. But to reach her, they need to first navigate Leicester Square, which is currently teeming with revellers. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: A report on the people fighting back against thieves stealing their mail and their identities. Plus, the parents facing huge phone bills racked up by their unsuspecting children

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Haslington near Crewe, Maryport in Cumbria, and Derby, and discuss plans with the new owners

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: How groundbreaking research into the psychology of crowds could help prevent attacks on police. Plus, a report on a surge in coughing attacks on police officers

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Following ambulance crews in Aberdeen and Edinburgh, as paramedics respond to a major crash on the M8. Elsewhere, an elderly lady needs treatment for a facial injury

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is at the helm on today's Bargain Hunt, which comes from Newark antiques fair. Today's experts are Paul Laidlaw and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem is left feeling isolated as more and more people sign up for New Trefelin. An act of sabotage breaks the village's calm, and Basil wastes no time in telling Emlyn who he thinks is to blame. Meanwhile, Sian decides that Ceri might be worth ensnaring with her feminine charms

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads on a house hunting mission in rural Devon for a couple who are looking for their first home together, with a budget of £525,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a tranquil garden in Newport, Wales, which must be suitable for growing herbs and practicing meditation

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including movie posters, Scandinavian furniture and some unusual dolls' heads. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Watford for a couple who long for an outdoor space to remind them of their former garden in Africa

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a bass guitar that once topped the charts in the hands of Tony Wilson, founding member of 1970s funk legends Hot Chocolate, but has since fallen into disrepair. Other items include a vintage end-of-the-pier machine, a carved wooden plaque, and a hand-carved belt that marked an unlikely wartime friendship

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A call comes in from London Fire Brigade requesting assistance at a fire in a block of flats which immediately puts the team under pressure. Meanwhile, mother and daughter team Nicola and Maisie have just finished with their first patient of the night but are immediately dispatched to a 12-year-old boy who has had an unfortunate accident

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: When a therapist encourages her to address her fear of rejection, Darby recalls applying to boarding school to get her parents' attention in her teenage years

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Darby finds herself stuck in a despondent marriage as she reconnects with someone from her past. Starring Anna Kendrick and Zoe Chao

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: When Darby has a medical scare, her overbearing mom, Claudia, comes to town to help. Romantic comedy anthology series, starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A spate of burglaries at Christmas leads to a police community support officer and his colleague being attacked, as they track down and try to arrest the thief. A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later. Plus, a West Yorkshire police officer starts his retirement needing physiotherapy after being attacked

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks back at some of the most eclectic toys and games bought by the teams over the years, and offers advice on how to spot a bargain. Plus, Charlie Ross learns about an early board games manufacturer from Gloucester

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson takes a look at one of the oldest and rarest plants known to man, the Wollemi pine, sometimes called the fossil tree. George Anderson shows how to naturalise new bulbs in grass, and Carole Baxter, using techniques borrowed from her father, reviews her tomatoes and sees if her dad has it right

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. However, if they are Decimated, they will leave with nothing. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour introduces a round-up of action from the final two days of the European Tour's traditional flagship event at Wentworth's famous West Course. Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter provide the commentary

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Martyn Colbeck and Cynthia Moss examine the legacy of Echo the elephant, who died in 2009 after being followed on film during the final chapter of her life, having guided her family for half a century. With a drought devastating the animals' home in the shadow of Kilimanjaro, a 38-strong band of relatives and descendants must overcome the loss of their leader, hunger and the threat of poachers to survive. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott celebrates dishes from across the Channel, getting inspiration from France, Italy and Spain. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' linguine, James Martin's Spanish sardines, a chicken dish from Raymond Blanc, and a Bake Off masterclass on how to make a classic Greek spanakopita

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Glemham Hall in the Suffolk countryside. David Fletcher discovers scripts from the TV series Lovejoy, and Jonathan Pratt spots Beatles dolls

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The quiz returns, with Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone going toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Skipper Paul notices that his boat is on a collision course with another trawler off the Scottish coast, and takes evasive action to ensure that the boats and nets avoid each other. Meanwhile, in the remote Norwegian sector of the North Sea, skipper Jonny McLeman goes for one last haul of fish and then starts the 24-hour journey back to Peterhead

programme.title: Rob Burrow: My Year with MND
programme.desc: Documentary following the former England and Leeds Rhino rugby league player as he deals with motor neurone disease, with contributions from family and friends. As the disease takes its toll physically, Burrow speaks of his determination to live as normal a life as possible, and explains how his rugby career has prepared him for the challenge

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer question on the specialist subjects of Manchester United, the novels of Toni Morrison, Berthe Morisot, and William Tyndale, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as the Puzzle Hunters take on the Walruses. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Mile High Club, Subway Footlongs, Edinburgh's Royal Mile, and A Miner's Inch

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The last first-round match sees four students from Magdalene College, Cambridge take on a team from University College, Oxford for a place in the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Nearly five years on from Jim Foley's execution at the hands of Jihadi John, Diane Foley is fighting for justice, while the Muellers continue their search for answers about their missing daughter Kayla. In London a court case in the UK Supreme Court threatens Diane's campaign to see the suspects face a trial in the US, while the Muellers are conducting their own investigation, supported by contacts working secretly in Syria

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: James Acaster, Tom Allen, Ed Byrne, Rhys James and Ellie Taylor join host Dara O Briain and regular participant Hugh Dennis for the topical comedy show, looking back at the week's events through the medium of fast-thinking and improvisation

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Being Blacker (2018)
programme.desc: Molly Dineen's documentary about Blacker Dread, a Jamaican-born reggae producer, businessman, father, son and pillar of the community in Brixton. The film focuses on a tumultuous time in his life as he deals with the death of his mother and the prospect of his first prison sentence

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A 999 call for a baby having a fit is a priority call, and crewmates Kate and Terry are immediately dispatched. They are 2.5 miles from the Category One emergency, but the traffic jams lead to a fraught journey for the crew. Trainee call handler Mackenzie, who is on her final shift of training, is put to the test when she takes a call from a woman in late-stage labour with twins

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: The return of the documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty. Police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight when a moped chase ends in chaos

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special edition of the programme exploring what it takes to police Britain's roads, including footage of a Metropolitan Police operation to tackle moped-enabled crime

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of wine, and a retro tin toy

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don revels in the early autumn colour in his garden, reviews how the tomatoes he grew from a late sowing have got on this season and gives advice on harvesting and storing apples. Rachel de Thame reveals how the new garden project she started in the spring has developed over the summer and Fish, of the rock band Marillion, gives a tour of his garden and reveals how it has helped his mental health as well as the creative process of making music. Plus, in Germany, Arit Anderson finds out about the transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Warrington Wolves v Wigan Warriors, Wakefield Trinity v St Helens and Leeds Rhinos v Castleford Tigers

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Fine art dealer Gavin Claxton, market trader Gary Barton and aeronautical engineer and part-time upcycler Stu Abbott head to Durham to find some bargains they can turn into some flipping profit. As Stu gets mechanical, making money from bits and parts of a Mini, Gavin goes to auction to try to sell his pricey painting, and Gary uses all the Glasgow patter he can muster to sell his art deco bowls in the city's Barras market

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After Woody sees George dancing with Tracy and gets the wrong end of the stick, he hands George an unitemised bill for $40,000 instead of telling him how he feels. It's left to George to work out what's really going on and try to make Woody and Tracy say what normally goes unsaid

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Having settled into his new role as host, Joe Lycett returns, along with the nine remaining home-sewers, who this time are tasked with making children's clothes. Challenges include creating hoodies and turning fake fur coats into fancy dress beasts. The final task sees contestants up against the clock as they make made-to-measure dance costumes so the dancing kids can perform The Hornpipe, the Tarantella and Highland dancing down the catwalk. However, one of them will be shown the door at the end of the show

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan travels to remote regions on three continents to learn how tribal cultures hunt and live alongside dangerous animals, beginning his journey in Ecuador, where he meets a family from the Waorani tribe. They venture into the Amazon Jungle, where the Waorani hunt monkeys and wild pigs using blowpipes and sharpened sticks, and try to trap an anaconda, an animal of great spiritual significance

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Christina Trevanion are in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, to assess a range of items, while presenter Paul Martin pays a visit to Nostell Priory near Wakefield

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Documentary about life in the port of Peterhead, following the people working on land and at sea to put fish on the nation's plates. Skipper James West heads out in search of prawns in his new £2million trawler the Westro, while in nearby Fraserburgh, scientist Aisling joins the crew of the Zenith to collect data for the Scottish Fishermen's Federation. James Cosmo narrates

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Diversity dancers Jordan Banjo and Perri Kiely join experts James Braxton and Phil Serrell on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. The celebrity pair discover an ancient Chinese leisure activity on a river in Cambridgeshire, before heading to their final destination at an auction in Greenwich

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Essex, the crew at Walton and Frinton gets an unusual mayday call to a ship called HMS Pickle. Arriving on scene, they discover it is not the modern naval vessel they expected, but a replica of a ship that once served in Nelson's fleet, and though the original survived the battle of Trafalgar, this one is shipping water and sinking fast. On the east coast of Scotland, the crew at Anstruther faces a tricky rescue trying to extract a walker with a badly broken leg from a remote coastal path. And in the Shannon basin in Ireland, the volunteers at one of the RNLI's few inland stations at Lough Derg are woken in the early hours of the morning by their pagers - cries for help have been heard coming from the 40-mile-long lake. After launching into inky darkness and thick fog, the crew make an eerie discovery

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons at Royal Papworth work to completely rebuild the chest wall of a patient who had to have a cancerous lung removed, and has lived with a hole in his back ever since. Consultant thoracic surgeon Aman Coonar and plastic surgeon Charles Malata must take a flap of muscle and skin from Kevin's back and painstakingly attach the blood vessels to fill the cavity and plug the hole. At Addenbrooke, a leading neurosurgeon must remove a potentially fatal tumour from the back of a patient's brain

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at 400 years of human trafficking from Africa to the New World, from the perspective of three different storylines. In the first episode, the actor travels to Gabon on the west coast of central Africa, where captives were herded onto ships

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at 400 years of human trafficking from Africa to the New World, from the perspective of three different storylines. The team continue their detailed examination of how 12 million Africans were shipped to the Americas

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian offers his unique take on different TV genres, beginning with a guide to the history of soap operas. Harry touches on everything from The Grove Family and The Newcomers to EastEnders and Coronation Street, while also giving viewers a few helpful tips on how to make their own domestic drama

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A shocking discovery by two unarmed police officers brings a bomb disposal unit to a suburb of Merseyside. In Dorset, a patient lashes out at a paramedic on the way to hospital

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Special episode looking at knife crime in the UK, including a dedicated police task force in Essex and a groundbreaking scheme in the West Midlands

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze race horse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and greengrocer Chris Bavin return to help more families with their food shopping, beginning with the Macbeth family in Windsor. Single mum Holly has her hands full looking after her two sons - 16-year-old Spencer and Fletcher, who is 11. Caring for Spencer, who has cerebral palsy, leaves Holly little time to cook, and as a result the family has become dependent on microwavable ready meals. While Spencer is happy to eat most foods, Fletcher - who has recently been diagnosed with autism - is fussier

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Art expert Kate Bliss, trader Linda Lambert and upholsterer Anthony Devine scour the antique shops, scrapyards and flea markets of Monmouth in search of items to make them a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: A car discovered at the bottom of a ravine and is found to contain the body of Bill's long-lost wife Trina. George wants to investigate, but Big Mac stands in his way

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The eight remaining home sewers attempt to revive the glamorous, rebellious and sometimes ridiculous, style of the 1970s. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to create flared jeans, punk-inspired outfits, and maxi dresses. Joe Lycett presents

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan joins a group of Bushmen in Botswana's Kalahari Desert to learn their ancient secrets for surviving on foot among wild lions. They teach him how to make fire, find honey, and gather water from desert plants, and the filmmaker watches as they hunt with poisoned arrows and track predatory lions

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot discovers a valuable sculpture at Rochester Cathedral in Kent and Raj Bisram finds a ghostly Victorian photo album

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Westro skipper James West and his crew have been in the North Sea for three days hunting prawns, but so far have struggled to find a catch so he decides to travel 120 miles in the hope of more luck. New recruit Gavin Burnett heads out on Peterhead trawler Ocean Endeavour, with the 17-year-old being given the responsibilty of bringing the fish on board and cooking for the crew. On the night of the Fishing News Awards, James is nominated for Fisherman of the Year, while rookie Gavin is hoping to land the Trainee of the Year awar

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors David Bradley and Nigel Harman travel to Oxfordshire and Somerset in search of antiques, assisted by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Christina Trevanion. Nigel takes a shine to a domestic appliance of yesteryear, and David comes across an item that resonates with one of the characters he has played

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: In the final episode, Mary travels to the Scottish Highlands. Surrounded by magical scenery, snow-capped mountains and frost covered forests, Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a sausage and pepper hotpot, simple twice-baked souffles, and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: This time, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for low cost baking, using ingredients that do not break the bank or compromise on flavour. Dishes include fluffy onion pretzels, a potato rosti quiche, rhubarb and custard kisses, and her signature rich and indulgent chocolate fondant

programme.title: A Life on Screen
programme.desc: From his early days in comedy troupe The Jockeys of Norfolk, to his breakthrough role as Charles in Four Weddings and a Funeral, Hugh Grant has become one of Britain's best-loved stars, with hits including two Bridget Jones movies, Notting Hill, Love Actually, and Paddington 2. More recently, he has turned his talents to television in the acclaimed drama A Very English Scandal, playing notorious politician Jeremy Thorpe. Aside from the Bafta-winning actor discussing his career, friends and colleagues including Andie MacDowell, Richard Curtis, Sandra Bullock, Colin Firth, Ang Lee, Stephen Frears and Nicholas Hoult also add their thoughts

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Everyone is delighted when Kevin books a cottage for them to spend half term in the countryside with all their children. But the house is not as nice as the pictures online suggested, and it is not long before tempers begin to fray. Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Paul Ready star

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at Fogo Island Inn, which is sited off the coast of Newfoundland in Canada and pays homage to the settlers who arrived from Ireland and Devon

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Nearly five years on from Jim Foley's execution at the hands of Jihadi John, Diane Foley is fighting for justice, while the Muellers continue their search for answers about their missing daughter Kayla. In London a court case in the UK Supreme Court threatens Diane's campaign to see the suspects face a trial in the US, while the Muellers are conducting their own investigation, supported by contacts working secretly in Syria

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Irish resort of Skerries, the crew run to the station when strong winds sweep a teenager on a paddleboard towards the busy shipping lanes of the Irish Sea, while in the Bristol Channel, among the many yachts taking advantage of a fresh summer breeze, one loses a crew member overboard. While the crowds are heading home at the end of a busy summer's day in Cleethorpes, two 13-year-old girls find themselves alone, cut off and at risk of drowning on a sandbank rapidly disappearing under a fast-rising tide

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Chris Wilson re-examines the case of Harold Shipman, exploring how attitudes to the elderly enabled the GP to get away with the murder of hundreds of patients. In the first instalment, he reveals how the doctor's crimes were first discovered and how he was finally apprehended and charged with the murder of 15 of his elderly patients.

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters are shocked when they are attacked by the residents of a house on fire as they try to put it out, while West Midlands Police officers patrol Birmingham on Black Friday

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: As part of the BBC's Museum Passion season, Cerys Matthews is given an access-all-areas pass to St Fagans National Museum of History in Cardiff, which is one of the UK's most visited heritage attractions and is a living history museum that embodies the culture and identity of Wales

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a creek slicing its way across a landscape of colour, in a setting dreams are made of.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould return for one final commission, helping Under-Sheriff and Secondary of London Charles Henty investigate whether two paintings in his possession are as valuable as he thinks. The first is a landscape picture of Dedham in Essex, thought to have been painted by pre-eminent British artist Alfred Munnings, and the second painting is of a medieval French village, and was rumoured to have been produced by part-time painter, full-time prime minister Winston Churchill. Charles hopes the sale of these two potentially pricey heirlooms will help protect jobs at a working French farm he inherited from his late uncle, but first he must learn whether the pictures are truly genuine

programme.title: Drama Out of a Crisis: A Celebration of Play for Today
programme.desc: Marking the 50th anniversary of the first Play For Today, this film celebrates the acclaimed series of controversial single dramas broadcast on BBC One between 1970 and 1984, told by a number of its producers, directors and writers

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: The wealthy Carlions gather for a christening, but it is 1945 and there are plans to be made, family wealth to protect and peace to be won. Drama by Trevor Griffiths, directed by Richard Eyre and starring Leo McKern, James Fox, Wendy Hiller and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: The first of four profiles marking Black History Month. Ellen E Jones narrates an exploration of the Get Out writer and director's career as a comedian, film-maker and producer in movies and TV, looking at how a lifelong love for the horror genre has informed his style

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Corrina Antrobus explores the work of the film director, who was the first black woman to win Best Director at Sundance, for her romantic drama Middle of Nowhere

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: Dublin-born social historian Emma Dabiri uses artworks held in the Ulster Museum in Belfast to explore a different way of seeing the world - through the eyes of artists

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: The day's sunlight is almost gone, but American artist Bob Ross finds what is left of it with an unusual blend of late-afternoon tones

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the UK, starting with the west of Britain. Fiinds include the lost First World War training trenches on Salisbury Plain, and Britain's first double henge - discovered just down the road from Stonehenge

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Part one of two. A real-life undercover thriller about two ordinary men who embark on an outrageously dangerous 10-year mission to penetrate the world's most secretive and brutal dictatorship - North Korea

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Part two of two. The continuing story of two men who embark on a mission to penetrate the world's most secretive and brutal dictatorship - North Korea

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Documentary from Grace Barber-Plentie on trailblazing film director Barry Jenkins, whose Best Picture winner Moonlight made Oscar night history in 2017 - beating La La Land to Best Picture following a dramatic and unprecedented envelope mix-up

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Documentary from Kambole Campbell exploring the work of Ryan Coogler, director of Fruitvale Station, Creed and the box-office sensation Black Panther

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people

programme.title: Black Nurses: The Women Who Saved the NHS
programme.desc: The story of the thousands of Caribbean and African women who answered the call more than 70 years ago to come to the UK and help build the National Health Service. Told by the women themselves, the documentary reveals the price they paid in leaving behind their families, and examines their struggle to overcome racism and their fight for career progression, as well as their cultural impact here and abroad

programme.title: Upstream
programme.desc: Over three years, cameras follow the course of the river Dee in Scotland to its source in the Cairngorm mountains, the highest of any river in Britain. The film, by writer Robert Macfarlane and director Rob Petit, begins in the lowland floodplains of the Dee, before travelling up, against the flow of the river, into Britain's own Arctic wilderness

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: Dublin-born social historian Emma Dabiri uses artworks held in the Ulster Museum in Belfast to explore a different way of seeing the world - through the eyes of artists

programme.title: Inside Museums
programme.desc: Artist Lachlan Goudie visits the Scottish National Gallery to explore the relevance of collection to the challenges people face today in terms of family, community, nature and hope

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint a rugged group of mountains peering down upon nature's greenery

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts how Edward I turned the castle into an instrument of colonisation. He spent a large amount of money on subduing the Welsh and built fearsome fortresses in Caernarfon, Gwynedd and Beaumaris, which were used to impose England's rule on the country. However, when the king's attention turned to Scotland, his plans didn't go as expected

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Comedy, written and directed by Mike Leigh, following the events of a party held in a suburban house. As the night wears on, the guests become increasingly inebriated and indiscreet, paving the way for shocking revelations and home truths. Alison Steadman, Tim Stern, John Salthouse and Janine Duvitski star

programme.title: Drama Out of a Crisis: A Celebration of Play for Today

programme.title: Abducted - Elizabeth I's Child Actors
programme.desc: Thirteen year-old Thomas Clifton was walking to school in December 1600 when he was violently kidnapped by men who claimed that they had legal authority to do so from Elizabeth I herself. Katherine Rundell examines the case, finding that the snatching of Thomas had been organised by a theatrical impresario who intended to put him on the stage as part of a company of child actors

programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends – Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two – the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda worries that Mick is being distant so goes to find Tina, who reassures her that his behaviour has nothing to do with her. Ben shares his concerns with Callum about Phil seeing Raymond, and what will happen if Denise finds out - but unknown to them, Lexi overhears their conversation. Gray encourages Whitney to tell the truth in her final statements, while Jean and Kat are shocked by a photo of Ruby and Martin on social media, and try to keep the news from Stacey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Birmingham, recalling the colourful characters he met while working the door at Digbeth's hottest night spots. He then introduces three guests, with Bella Hull making her TV debut with some excellent time saving tips, Texas-born Kemah Bob discussing the differences between Americans and Brits, and Chris Washington filling the audience in on lockdown fitness pursuits

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The neighbours all confront a truth in their life. David investigates Kelly's past, unsure of what he's hoping to find. Can he assuage his doubts, or will he always fear that she wasn't the woman he thought she was? An argument with Liam propels Hannah toward Andy, but their unexpected moment of intimacy is interrupted. Belle's ex-husband Neil walks back into her life and tests her loyalty to Maya, on the night when Maya needs her most. Gail finally tells Henry her true feelings about their relationship in an explosive confrontation, which dissects their marriage from its very beginnings

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda worries that Mick is being distant so goes to find Tina, who reassures her that his behaviour has nothing to do with her. Ben shares his concerns with Callum about Phil seeing Raymond, and what will happen if Denise finds out - but unknown to them, Lexi overhears their conversation. Gray encourages Whitney to tell the truth in her final statements, while Jean and Kat are shocked by a photo of Ruby and Martin on social media, and try to keep the news from Stacey

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they can't resist getting involved with Thomas's tale as well

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Birmingham, recalling the colourful characters he met while working the door at Digbeth's hottest night spots. He then introduces three guests, with Bella Hull making her TV debut with some excellent time saving tips, Texas-born Kemah Bob discussing the differences between Americans and Brits, and Chris Washington filling the audience in on lockdown fitness pursuits

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda helps a guilty Gray by picking up the kids from school and cleaning up his home - only to get on his bad side by touching Chantelle's favourite mug. In the meantime, Whitney's QC is taken ill at court, meaning there is only one other person who can step up for the final statements. Lola questions Ben about Phil's relationship to Raymond - but when he tells her to stay out of it, she feels torn, and later decides to tell Denise. Stacey finds herself in an embarrassing situation

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Rory's shock at meeting someone from his past inadvertently makes him the hero, and Maggie worries about Caitlin while trying to keep Ruby calm. Poppy reveals her fears, and Bob wonders if he's going to be granted a second chance at happiness.

programme.title: The Children's Hospital
programme.desc: Paediatric surgeon Mr Patel performs an operation to return a baby's bowel to his body. The Emergency team battles to remove an unusual object stuck inside a boy's nose

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The neighbours all confront a truth in their life. David investigates Kelly's past, unsure of what he's hoping to find. Can he assuage his doubts, or will he always fear that she wasn't the woman he thought she was? An argument with Liam propels Hannah toward Andy, but their unexpected moment of intimacy is interrupted. Belle's ex-husband Neil walks back into her life and tests her loyalty to Maya, on the night when Maya needs her most. Gail finally tells Henry her true feelings about their relationship in an explosive confrontation, which dissects their marriage from its very beginnings

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Thirty minutes into the night shift, police request urgent assistance at the scene of an incident - a man has called to say his mum has been stabbed.
